english: Runebanger could hardly comprehend what he'd just witnessed. Suddenly it all made sense. Why Smoulder and Empress Jenny had been willing to forget years of hostilities. Why they suddenly spoke so positively about the "potential" of an alliance between charr and humans. He clutched his mane as furious tears matted his cheeks. How could Smoulder even LOOK at Jenny that way? What about her furless figure could possible appeal to rugged, tough, well-groomed charr such as they? Burn it all, the mouse didn't even have horns! Did no one appreciate the gentle curve of horns anymore?"Smoulder's place is by MY side, as MY ally, as MY confidant, as MY intimate...as my..."No. Brood Legion would not stand for this. Not if Runebanger had his way.
Running into them will dismount and briefly immobilize you.

Rustbane,This rat infestation is out of hand. Do NOT let those critters get into the food supply, and don't hesitate to exterminate them if they come into Hullgarden—they're obviously infected with some kind of plague. This is the last thing we need! As if it wasn't hard enough to gather food and survive around here...Do your best, and I'll see if I can rustle up some help for you. Somebody needs to figure out where these damned things are coming from! Didn't you say you had some thoughts about that?—Osborne
Rytlock and Caithe have gone into the dark heart of Twilight Arbor to confront Caithe's former lover, Faolain—who is now the head of the Nightmare Court.
Rytlock and Caithe have gone into the dark heart of Twilight Arbor to confront her former companion, Faolain, now the head of the Nightmare Court.

Rytlock and the orders of Tyria need more information about the necromancer Rissa's amulets. The Order of Whispers is standing by to steal the information they need from the Inquest, and Korukhan the Blacksmith is standing by for you to join their efforts.

Rytlock asked me to check in on the Cragstead situation. When I arrived in Hoelbrak to meet Braham, I walked in on a pretty...colorful chat between him, Knut, and Eir. And here I thought a war council of battle-hungry Tribunes was intimidating.The Molten Alliance has been striking small villages throughout the Shiverpeaks—including Cragstead, Braham's home. Even though a raid was imminent, Knut was reluctant to send any reinforcements. They've got enough of a refugee crisis on their doorstep, same as the charr in the Citadel. Given the way things escalated at the hatchery, I volunteered to join Braham in fighting back.
Rytlock Brimstone is a famous, highly influential tribune, as well as one of the fiercest fighters among the charr. If he's summoned you, there must be a compelling reason.
Rytlock Brimstone is a highly influential tribune and one of the fiercest fighters among the charr. If he's summoned you, there must be a compelling reason.

Rytlock Brimstone is itching for some action, so he's joining you on the hunt for your father's treasure. With the tribune by your side, any Grendich Ruins ghosts that stand in your way are in for the fight of their afterlives.

Rytlock Brimstone provided the location of the Flame Legion's camp, but Tribune Desertgrave doesn't want Blood interfering in this Ash operation. She's ordered you into the camp to learn the Flame Legion's plans. Only then are you free to rescue any Blood Legion prisoners you may find.
Rytlock Brimstone, Tribune of the Blood Legion

S,    This is the last letter I wanted to write, and I'm certain it'll never find its way to you... I just wanted to surprise you, S. I wanted to live and grow old with you, but I don't think I'm ever getting out of this chamber. My tomb. The Unseen Ones—these foul, awful beings that we worshipped—they're going to sacrifice me next, after all we did for them.    Just before we were brought here, I found you a ring, and I'd stashed it in my pocket. They never took it from me when we arrived, which surprised me. It belonged to one of the villagers, before the twins... Before they killed all of them. I found it in the pile of trinkets that they kept. Is that awful of me, after all we did to those people? Is my desperation so cruel? It's not like it matters anyway. The Unseen Ones brought me to this chamber and locked me inside. I'm alone in here, and I'm...I'm not getting out. Whenever I sleep, I hear whispers. When I wake up, I feel weaker. I don't know what kind of torture this is, but the subtlety is maddening. They left me paper to write and a little bit of food to eat... It's as if they don't want me to die, not yet, but they do want me to suffer.    Just before they put me in here, I managed to hide your ring under a bush while their backs were turned—just outside of the gate to the lakeside holding facility. Was it west of here? My memory worsens by the hour. The room feels smaller... And while writing this... There's just no way you'll find my letter. In fact, I hope you don't. I can only hope now that your death is kinder than mine. I hope you never see this place.    Knowing what I know now... Instead of burying the ring, I should've placed it near the wall where we last kissed. We crossed that very spot while they escorted me to my fate. Do you remember that day, S? When they brought us all to the lake and let us swim and bathe? It was as if they wanted us to feel joy, albeit briefly, so that our suffering would feel all the worse... Gods, have mercy. Forgive us.    Even if I die here...I'll always wait for you. I just wish we had more time. I hope your death... I just don't want you to feel any pain.    —V
Sabetha has returned to the platform.

Sabil,Leave your wife. She's dead weight, and we have business.We'll meet you on the road south of the farms. Our master's unhappy with your progress, so he's sent us to make sure all goes as planned. We have more of the special eggs he wants delivered. A few in each house should do the trick.Be swift. He expects much of you.

Salutations!Tales of your exploits in Caledon Forest have spread far and wide, and one thing has become exceedingly clear: your determination and skill are unmatched. There's a whole world out there, waiting for someone like you.Keep up the good work.— Tyrian Explorers Society

Salutations!Word of your exploits continues to spread far and wide. No matter where you travel, you embrace those around you and help them in their times of need. Lornar's Pass has long been a challenge for travelers and adventurers alike, but you overcame all its unique trials with ease.But as your forays into the unfamiliar expand, be aware that the dangers will likely increase as well. It has been said that the most enduring legends are built upon foundations of danger.Keep up the good work.—Tyrian Explorers Society

Salutations, adventurer! Across Tyria, many have heard of the terror of the Molten Alliance that plagued the northlands—and now, a new opportunity has come to let you revisit their downfall firsthand!That's right, in the Consortium's proprietary Fractals of the Mists, you can relive pivotal moments in Tyria's history within Mists-based simulacrum environments*. Accompany up-and-coming heroes into the subterranean lair of the Molten Alliance, rescue their prisoners, and escape their dastardly overseers!Fabulous experiences and exciting prizes await in the Fractals of the Mists! Try it today in Lion's Arch!—Your friends at the Consortium*Fractal recreations are not guaranteed to be precisely accurate to historic events. Terms and conditions apply; see attached adventurer agreement for details and liability waiver.(You received this message as a party of interest in events that have been identified as appearing in Fractal recreations. If you wish to unsubscribe from future Consortium offers and opportunities, please submit a notarized letter of disinterest (Form-3C-D) to our Lion's Arch offices for processing.)

Salutations, citizens of Tyria! For too long, Tyrians have unknowingly toiled beneath cruel and distant overlords—concealing themselves high above, out of reach, sight, and accountability. Yes, you are all too aware of whom I speak: tall people.But with the latest wonders of science and ingenuity, we need not fear! My latest creation will let all equally experience the high life, just as soon as I get it past the absurd and out-of-touch regulators at the Rata Sum Bureau of Potions, Reagents, and Explosives.Don't delay another second! Meet me in Lion's Arch and participate in the completely safe and healthful live-fire testing today! —Professor Smoll

Salutations, favored test subject!I have heard through the intercollegiate gossip data repositories that you are embarking on an entrepreneurial endeavor of your own. Of course, I wish to offer you all the best and my full support and would not wish to distract you for a moment!HOWEVER, If you should need extra flasks of my world-renowned height-adjustment tonics, or any other mysterious curios that I have in stock but do not claim to personally be the originator, manufacturer, or legally liable entity of, don't hesitate to stop by my pop-up shop in Lion's Arch.Just look for all the dogs! I'm right next to the big one!—Professor Smoll

Salutations, favored test subject!Once again, I have opened my pop-up shop in the fine city of Lion's Arch to supply its eager populace with my tonics, reagents and novel arcana. Potions to change your outlook on life are flying off the shelves, and appreciation for the whimsical sweeps the city!As my brightest patron and live-trial participant, you may be particularly interested in my latest creation, the Fool's Ocular Tonic! One sip will open your worldview to new understanding, allowing you to see yourself and your fellows as though your eyes were once again unclouded by the years of doubts, suffering, and heartwrenching heroic endeavor. Plus, it's got a neat taste!Just look for all the dogs! I'm right next to the big one!—Professor Smoll

Salutations, fellow wanderer,I keep hearing reports of your bravery and skill. It's impressive. Your deeds in Shaemoor, at both the village and the garrison, are commendable. You are a credit to the art and craft of cartography. —Tyrian Explorers Society

Salutations, I am writing to thank you for lending a hand. It never fails to amaze me how helpful a perfect stranger can be in dark times. People like you are the true substance of the world. The weapons testing you did was a monumental help to me, and I'm sure you already realize how vital your work with the misfiring turrets was. I am grateful.—Tactician Lyan

Salutations, Wintersday celebrants!After touring the cities of Tyria, Tixx the Toymaker and his Funwerks Krewe have finally arrived in Lion's Arch! Festivities are in full-swing, with wassailing and singing, and even bell ringing. But much is astir in Tixx's airship. Now overrun with malfunctioning toys, a Seraph guard is recruiting adventurers to cull the hordes and secure the aerial lab.But the excitement won't last forever—Tixx is only in Lion's Arch for two weeks, and once he leaves his airship and toys go with him. So don't be a lollygagger! Make sure to stop by the Grand Piazza, where Tixx has a special gift just for you (one that, we've been assured, won't talk back).—Captain Tokk

Salutations, You will be happy to know that my research is proceeding apace, thanks in no small part to your recent timely assistance. The specimens which you procured have proven to be invaluable as I seek to produce the ultimate skale poison. It is rare to make the acquaintance of a soldier who has such an appreciation for the value of research that they are willing to risk their lives to further its pursuit. You're a good egg.—The Highly Respected and Accomplished Researcher Irkz
Salutations,A trip through the centaur homeland is no small feat. Safe havens are few and far between, and yet you pressed boldly onward into the heart of danger. Bravo!—Tyrian Explorers Society

Salutations,Dry Top's arid terrain is unknown to many. Your successful charting of the area speaks to your outstanding abilities and grit. Facing sandstorms, quicksand, and dust mites is a harrowing prospect, but you've braved them all.We eagerly await news of your future exploits.—Tyrian Explorers Society

Salutations,From what I hear, the talks at Summit Peak are going well. The Separatists and Renegades are still trying their best to cause trouble, but we've been able to keep them in check on our front. Of course, that wouldn't have been possible without the strength and resourcefulness you displayed on The Siege Plains. I hope this letter finds you well and that your success here encourages you to continue in kind.—Lieutenant Marrel

Salutations,I hope this letter reaches you, as you seem to be quite the traveler. It seems you have touched the lives of many within Snowden Drifts. From skaalds to hunters, Lionguard and respected elders, all that speak your name do so with respect and gratefulness. Thank you for lending a hand to these people.—Tyrian Explorers Society

Salutations,It seems you've not only traveled the entirety of Brisban Wildlands, but also assisted others along the way. You've demonstrated a talent for exploration and predilection for charity. These are the very qualities our society was founded upon. In light of this accomplishment, we give you this gift.—Tyrian Explorers Society

Salutations,Pardon the subject line, but I couldn't resist. I'm pleased to report that the drake situation is becoming somewhat managable. We're having fewer reports of drakes attacking travelers, which means I shouldn't have to sentence my Lionguard to certain doom on drake extermination detail. Surely we wouldn't have made such progress without you.—Lionguard Tunnira

Salutations,Reports of your endeavors throughout the Straits of Devastation have reached the Tyrian Explorers Society. Your willingness to venture through those perilous lands to aid the war against Zhaitan is notably impressive. You've shown great courage, and we commend you.We excitedly await details of your further exploits wherever your travels may take you.— Tyrian Explorers Society

Salutations,Southsun Cove may be an island of rare natural wonders, but its unique fauna has proven challenging to explorer and settler alike. We salute your courage and willingness to venture into such an inhospitable territory.Keep up the good work.—Tyrian Explorers Society

Salutations,The Durmand Priory, Vigil, and Order of Whispers have long reported on their daily struggles getting their troops safely across the harsh territory of Malchor's Leap. However, they recently sent word on your time in that area.
They tell us that you rose to this challenge, blazing your own trails to the undead lurking there. It seems you have helped solve many of those troop-transport challenges.Due to the historical significance of Orr, we at the Tyrian Explorers Society look upon your recent exploits with both admiration and envy. You are truly an inspiration to your peers.— Tyrian Explorers Society

Salutations,Your efforts in the Forsaken Thicket are the stuff of legend. It has come to my attention that you have assembled a very remarkable set of armor, and I would like to offer my expertise to help improve it to make a truly legendary creation.Find me in Lion's Arch.—Giita
